title: 10 best herbal tinctures for vomiting
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for vomiting are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, prepared by soaking plant material in a solvent such as alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures can help alleviate vomiting by addressing the underlying causes, such as digestive issues, motion sickness, or food poisoning. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with vomiting include ginger, chamomile, and licorice root. Ginger, for instance, is well-known for its anti-emetic properties and can help soothe an upset stomach, while chamomile has calming effects on the digestive system and can help reduce inflammation. Licorice root can help protect the stomach lining and reduce irritation. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from vomiting and improve their overall quality of life, particularly those prone to motion sickness or digestive issues.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with vomiting because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. It also stimulates the production of digestive juices, which can help regulate digestion and reduce the likelihood of vomiting. Additionally, yarrow tincture has been shown to have antispasmodic effects on the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which can help alleviate the muscle spasms that can lead to vomiting. Its calming effect on the stomach can provide relief from nausea and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Yarrow tincture can help reduce vomiting by acting as an anti-emetic, which helps to calm the stomach and suppress nausea, thanks to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and sesquiterpene lactones.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture has astringent properties that can help to tighten and tone the tissues of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the likelihood of vomiting, due to the tannins found in the plant.

Carminative: Yarrow tincture can help to alleviate vomiting caused by gas and bloating by promoting the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, thanks to the presence of volatile oils like chamazulene and alpha-pinene.
Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture can help to reduce vomiting caused by inflammation in the digestive tract, as it contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Anxiolytic: Yarrow tincture can help to reduce vomiting caused by anxiety or stress by promoting relaxation and reducing tension, which can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting, due to the presence of lactones and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of yarrow tincture plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for vomiting due to their astringent properties that help to soothe the digestive system.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves are used in tea to treat vomiting because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining.
Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting because they contain volatile oils that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce spasms.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are used in tea for vomiting because they contain antispasmodic and carminative properties that can help relieve digestive discomfort and reduce the frequency of vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully dried and free of moisture.
Chop the dried yarrow into small pieces and place them in a clean, dry jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped yarrow, ensuring the plant material is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.

Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the plant material, and store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple giant hyssop tincture helps with vomiting because it contains antispasmodic and carminative properties that soothe the digestive system and reduce the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. The herb's active compounds, including marrubiin and flavonoids, work together to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach. Additionally, purple giant hyssop has been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, making it a popular natural remedy for managing vomiting and other gastrointestinal discomforts.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic property: Purple giant hyssop tincture can help reduce vomiting by containing antiemetic compounds, such as marrubiin and diterpenes, that inhibit the vomiting reflex.

Carminative property: This tincture can alleviate vomiting caused by gastric distension, as it contains carminative agents like caryophyllene and pinene, which help expel excess gas from the digestive tract.

Antispasmodic property: The presence of antispasmodic constituents, like flavonoids and tannins, in purple giant hyssop tincture can help relax gastrointestinal muscles, thus reducing vomiting caused by spasms or cramps.

Anxiolytic property: Purple giant hyssop tincture can help manage vomiting triggered by anxiety or stress, thanks to its anxiolytic properties derived from compounds like pinene and flavonoids, which promote relaxation and reduce tension.

Antimicrobial property: This tincture can help combat vomiting caused by bacterial or viral infections, as it contains antimicrobial agents, such as marrubiin and flavonoids, that inhibit the growth of pathogens in the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help soothe digestion and alleviate vomiting.
Seeds: High in oil content, which can help lubricate the digestive tract and reduce vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh purple giant hyssop leaves and flowers, or measure out 1 cup of dried herb, and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Cover the jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a dark glass bottle or jar, squeezing out all the liquid from the herbs.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place; take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with vomiting because it contains a compound called thujone, which has been shown to help regulate gastrointestinal motility and reduce nausea and vomiting. The herb also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an irritated digestive tract and reduce inflammation that can contribute to vomiting. Additionally, wormwood has been used historically as a digestive tonic, and can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which can improve overall digestive function and reduce symptoms of vomiting. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before using wormwood tincture, as it can interact with certain medications and may not be appropriate for everyone.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Wormwood tincture can help reduce vomiting by containing therapeutic compounds, such as absinthin and anabsinthin, that help regulate gastrointestinal motility and reduce nausea.

Carminative: Wormwood tincture can help alleviate vomiting caused by gastric distension and discomfort by promoting the expulsion of intestinal gas and soothing the gastrointestinal tract with active constituents like essential oils and flavonoids.

Choleretic: Wormwood tincture can help manage vomiting caused by liver and gallbladder issues by stimulating the production and flow of bile, which contains active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones that can improve digestion and reduce vomiting.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tincture can help combat vomiting caused by bacterial or viral infections by containing active constituents like thujone, isothujone, and camphor that have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, helping to eliminate pathogens and reduce vomiting.

Nervine: Wormwood tincture can help alleviate vomiting caused by stress, anxiety, or neurological issues by acting as a mild sedative and nerve tonic, containing active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils that can calm the nervous system and reduce vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of wormwood tincture plant are used in making medicinal tea for vomiting due to their bitter properties that stimulate digestion and reduce nausea.

Flowers: The flowers of wormwood tincture plant are useful in making medicinal tea for vomiting as they contain volatile oils that have antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach.

Seeds: The seeds of wormwood tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for vomiting because they contain therapeutic compounds that can help alleviate gastrointestinal spasms and reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Bark: The bark of wormwood tincture plant is used in making medicinal tea for vomiting due to its astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wormwood herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the solid herbs and keeping the liquid.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, taking 1/4 teaspoon every few hours as needed for vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tincture helps with vomiting because it contains anti-emetic properties that aid in suppressing nausea and vomiting. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate digestive issues, including stomach aches, cramps, and bloating. Mugwort tincture works by stimulating the production of digestive juices, which helps regulate the digestive system and reduce the likelihood of vomiting. Additionally, mugwort has calming effects on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that can sometimes trigger vomiting. Overall, mugwort tincture can provide natural relief for those experiencing vomiting and other digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Mugwort tincture can help reduce vomiting by calming the stomach and reducing the urge to vomit, due to its active constituents such as volatile oils like thujone and camphor.
Carminative: It can alleviate vomiting caused by gastric distress by promoting the expulsion of gas from the stomach, thanks to its active constituents like volatile oils and flavonoids.
Antispasmodic: Mugwort tincture can help relieve vomiting caused by muscle spasms in the digestive tract, due to its active constituents like volatile oils and flavonoids.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that help calm the stomach and reduce nausea, making them effective in treating vomiting.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe the digestive system and alleviate vomiting.
Stems: Rich in essential oils and bitter compounds that stimulate digestion, helping to reduce vomiting and promote healthy digestion.
Rhizomes: Packed with therapeutic compounds, including volatile oils and flavonoids, that help regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh mugwort leaves and stems, or crumble 1 ounce of dried mugwort, and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the mugwort, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the desired steeping period, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out any excess liquid.

Store the finished mugwort tincture in a clean, amber glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. For vomiting, take 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of the tincture as needed. Consult with a healthcare provider for proper dosage and usage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tincture helps with vomiting because it contains high levels of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the herbal extract can help restore electrolyte balance and provide hydration, which is often lost during bouts of vomiting. This property is important for vomiting because it helps to alleviate the symptoms and discomfort associated with vomiting. The active constituents that give bell pepper its anti-emetic properties include capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin.

Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This property is important for vomiting because inflammation in the digestive tract can trigger vomiting. The active constituents that give bell pepper its anti-inflammatory properties include vitamin C, vitamin A, and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Bell pepper tincture has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for vomiting because free radicals can damage the digestive tract and trigger vomiting. The active constituents that give bell pepper its antioxidant properties include vitamin C, vitamin A, and flavonoids.

Carminative: Bell pepper tincture has carminative properties, which can help to relieve gas and bloating in the digestive tract. This property is important for vomiting because gas and bloating can trigger vomiting. The active constituents that give bell pepper its carminative properties include volatile oils such as limonene and pinene.

Diaphoretic: Bell pepper tincture has diaphoretic properties, which can help to promote sweating and eliminate toxins from the body. This property is important for vomiting because toxins in the body can trigger vomiting. The active constituents that give bell pepper its diaphoretic properties include vitamin C and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce vomiting.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils and have been shown to have antiemetic properties, making them useful for treating vomiting.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce vomiting and soothe the digestive system.
Stems: Have astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, thereby reducing vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Chop two bell peppers and one cup of anti-emetic herbs (such as ginger, licorice root, or peppermint), then combine in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the finished tincture in a glass dropper bottle, and administer 30 drops to 1 teaspoon every 4-6 hours as needed for vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coptis chinensis tincture helps with vomiting because it contains high levels of berberine, a powerful alkaloid that has been shown to have anti-emetic properties. This means that it can help to reduce the frequency and severity of vomiting by suppressing the vomiting reflex in the digestive tract. The herb has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat digestive disorders, including nausea and vomiting, and its effectiveness has been supported by a growing body of scientific research.

Gastroprotective: The tincture has gastroprotective properties due to its ability to inhibit gastric acid secretion and increase mucin production, reducing the likelihood of vomiting caused by gastric irritation.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Coptis chinensis are commonly used to make medicinal tea for vomiting due to their alkaloid content, which can help to reduce inflammation and nausea.

Flowers: The flowers of Coptis chinensis have been used in traditional medicine to treat vomiting, as they contain antioxidants that can help to soothe the digestive system.
Seeds: The seeds of this plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.

remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Coptis chinensis root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL as needed for vomiting.

Gastroprotective: Coca leaf tincture has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce the risk of vomiting caused by gastric irritation.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, amber glass dropper bottle for up to 1 year, using 1-2 dropperfuls as needed for vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tincture helps with vomiting because it contains alkaloids that have antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract and reduce the frequency and intensity of vomiting. Additionally, the tincture can help to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can improve digestion and reduce the likelihood of vomiting. The herb has been traditionally used to treat a variety of digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Yellow jessamine tincture can help reduce vomiting by containing alkaloids such as scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which act on the central nervous system to suppress nausea and vomiting.

Sedative: The tincture can also help alleviate vomiting by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, due to the presence of alkaloids like hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which have sedative properties.

Spasmolytic: Yellow jessamine tincture can help relieve vomiting caused by gastrointestinal spasms, as it contains alkaloids like hyoscyamine and atropine, which have antispasmodic effects on smooth muscles.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in alkaloids, the flowers of yellow jessamine are used to make medicinal tea for vomiting due to their antiemetic properties.
Leaves: Containing sedative and antispasmodic compounds, the leaves of yellow jessamine are used in medicinal tea to alleviate vomiting caused by digestive spasms.
Bark: The bark of yellow jessamine contains active compounds that help to soothe the stomach lining, making it useful in medicinal tea for vomiting.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of fresh yellow jessamine flowers and leaves, and rinse them gently to remove any dirt or impurities.
Chop the herbs finely and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, then add 10 oz of vodka or grain alcohol (at least 80 proof) to cover the herbs completely.
Seal the jar tightly and shake it gently to mix the herbs and alcohol, then store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it once a day.
After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out the excess liquid from the herbs.

Transfer the yellow jessamine tincture to a clean, amber glass bottle, label it with the name, date, and dosage, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Suggested dosage for vomiting: 1-2 mL (1/2-1 teaspoon) every 2-4 hours, or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Henbane tincture helps with vomiting because it contains alkaloids such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which have antispasmodic and antiemetic properties. These alkaloids work by suppressing the activity of the vagus nerve, which can trigger nausea and vomiting. Additionally, henbane tincture can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, further alleviating symptoms of vomiting. It is important to note that henbane tincture should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner, as it can be toxic in large doses.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Henbane tincture contains alkaloids such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which act on the central nervous system to reduce the urge to vomit, making it effective in treating nausea and vomiting.

remedy_parts: Seeds: Henbane seeds are used in medicinal tea for vomiting due to their anticholinergic properties that can help suppress excessive stomach acid production.
Leaves: Henbane leaves are often used in teas for vomiting because they contain alkaloids that can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract.
Flowers: Henbane flowers are used in medicinal tea to alleviate vomiting, as they contain compounds that can help reduce spasms in the digestive system.
Roots: Henbane roots are used in tea to treat vomiting, as they contain chemicals that can help slow down the movement of the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh Henbane (Hyoscyamus niger) leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Chop the herbs finely and fill a clean, dry glass jar up to half, then add 80-proof vodka until the jar is full, covering the herbs by at least 1 inch.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the recommended time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean, dry glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, the date, and the dosage (typically 1-2 mL up to three times a day), and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mexican morning glory tincture helps with vomiting because it contains a natural alkaloid called ergine, which is known to have anti-emetic properties. remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of dried Mexican morning glory seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 oz of vodka or rum over the seeds until they are fully submerged, then secure the lid.
Let the mixture steep for two to three weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and take 1/4 teaspoon every 4-6 hours as needed for vomiting.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat vomiting with herbal tinctures is by using ginger or chamomile. Ginger has been shown to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting, while chamomile is known for its anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach. Dilute 1-2 dropperfuls of the tincture in water and sip slowly every few hours. However, if vomiting persists for more than 24 hours or is accompanied by severe abdominal pain, fever, or other concerning symptoms, seek immediate medical attention.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to vomiting that are treated with herbal tinctures are nausea, indigestion, and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). Herbal tinctures like ginger, chamomile, and licorice have been used to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Ginger, for instance, is known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby reducing nausea and vomiting. Chamomile, on the other hand, has calming effects on the stomach muscles and can help reduce the frequency of vomiting. Licorice, meanwhile, can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract and reduce acid reflux, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking any herbal remedies, as they can interact with other medications and have potential side effects.
